supplements.name: Vitamin D3
supplements.shortDescription: Supports bone health, immune function, and mood regulation.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble vitamin — absorption is significantly higher when taken with a fatty meal.

synergyReasons.vitamin_k2: K2 directs calcium to bones rather than arteries — essential companion to D3.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium is required to activate Vitamin D in the body.
synergyReasons.omega3: Both are fat-soluble and share absorption pathways — taking together may improve bioavailability.
synergyReasons.probiotics: Combining probiotics with Vitamin D has synergistic effects on metabolic health and recovery.

interactionReasons.vitamin_a: High-dose preformed Vitamin A (retinol, >5,000 IU/day) antagonises Vitamin D receptor signalling and has been associated with reduced bone mineral density despite adequate D3 supplementation. The optimal A:D ratio is estimated at roughly 5:1 (IU). Keep retinol doses moderate when supplementing D3. (Johansson S, J Bone Miner Res 2004; Cannell JJ, Ann Otol Rhinol Laryngol 2008)
interactionReasons.st_johns_wort: St. John's Wort strongly induces CYP3A4, accelerating Vitamin D metabolism and reducing serum levels.

supplements.notes: Most people are deficient. Testing blood levels (25-OHD) before supplementing is recommended. Target range: 40–60 ng/mL.

supplements.name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
supplements.shortDescription: Directs calcium to bones and away from arteries. Essential companion to D3.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — best absorbed with a meal containing dietary fat.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: D3 increases calcium absorption; K2 ensures calcium goes to bones, not arteries.
synergyReasons.calcium: K2 activates osteocalcin, which binds calcium to the bone matrix.

interactionReasons.vitamin_e: High-dose Vitamin E (>300 IU/day) competitively inhibits vitamin K-dependent γ-carboxylase enzymes, impairing K2's ability to activate osteocalcin and matrix Gla protein. Keep Vitamin E doses moderate (<200 IU) when supplementing K2 for bone or cardiovascular benefits. (Booth SL, Am J Clin Nutr 2004)

supplements.notes: MK-7 form has a longer half-life (72 hours) and better bioavailability than MK-4. Those on warfarin should consult a doctor.

supplements.name: Vitamin C
supplements.shortDescription: Powerful antioxidant supporting immunity, collagen synthesis, and iron absorption.

timing.reason: Water-soluble and well-absorbed any time. Taking with iron-rich meals boosts non-heme iron absorption.

synergyReasons.iron: Vitamin C reduces ferric to ferrous iron, significantly enhancing non-heme iron absorption.
synergyReasons.collagen_peptides: Vitamin C is an essential cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase — required for collagen synthesis.

interactionReasons.vitamin_b12: High-dose Vitamin C (≥500 mg) can oxidatively degrade B12 in the digestive tract. Take at least 2 hours apart. (Herbert V, Am J Clin Nutr 1978; NIH ODS caution)
interactionReasons.selenium: High-dose Vitamin C can reduce the bioavailability of inorganic selenium (selenate form) by converting it to poorly absorbed elemental selenium. Prefer organic forms (selenomethionine) or take separately. (Oster O, J Nutr Biochem 1993)

supplements.notes: Water-soluble — excess is excreted in urine. UL is 2,000 mg/day. Buffered or liposomal forms are gentler on the stomach at higher doses.

supplements.name: Vitamin B12
supplements.shortDescription: Essential for energy metabolism, nerve function, and red blood cell formation.

timing.reason: Best taken in the morning as B12 supports alertness and energy metabolism.

synergyReasons.folate: B12 and folate are co-dependent for methylation, homocysteine regulation, and DNA synthesis.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b_complex: B12 works synergistically with other B vitamins for energy metabolism and methylation.

interactionReasons.vitamin_c: High-dose Vitamin C (≥500 mg) can oxidatively degrade B12 in the digestive tract. Take at least 2 hours apart. (Herbert V, Am J Clin Nutr 1978; NIH ODS caution)
interactionReasons.berberine: Berberine activates AMPK and inhibits intrinsic factor secretion — the same mechanism by which metformin impairs B12 absorption. Long-term combined use warrants B12 monitoring. (Hua W, Eur J Med Chem 2020; de Jager J, BMJ 2010)

supplements.notes: Methylcobalamin form is preferred for absorption. Vegans, vegetarians, and those over 50 are at highest deficiency risk. Intrinsic factor is required for absorption — those with pernicious anaemia may need injections.
sources: Allen LH, Am J Clin Nutr 2009

supplements.name: Magnesium Glycinate
supplements.shortDescription: Highly bioavailable magnesium form for relaxation, sleep quality, and muscle function.

timing.reason: Best taken in the evening — promotes relaxation and supports deep sleep.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Magnesium activates Vitamin D and is required for its conversion to the active form (calcitriol).
synergyReasons.zinc: Zinc and magnesium together (ZMA formulation) support testosterone levels and sleep quality.
synergyReasons.l_theanine: Both promote calm and relaxation without sedation — complementary mechanisms.

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters. Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium share intestinal absorption channels. High-dose co-administration reduces the uptake of both. Space by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975)

supplements.notes: Glycinate form is gentle on the stomach and has higher bioavailability than oxide or citrate. ~47% of the US population does not meet the EAR for magnesium. If taking multiple magnesium forms in a day, space doses by at least 6 hours.
sources: Abbasi B, J Res Med Sci 2012

supplements.name: Magnesium Malate
supplements.shortDescription: Energizing magnesium form that supports ATP production and reduces muscle fatigue.

timing.reason: Malate supports energy production via the Krebs cycle — best taken in the morning with food.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Magnesium activates Vitamin D and is required for its conversion to the active form.
synergyReasons.creatine: Both support ATP production and muscular performance through different mechanisms.

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters. Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium share intestinal absorption channels. High-dose co-administration reduces the uptake of both. Space by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975)

supplements.notes: Better choice than glycinate when daytime energy support is the primary goal rather than sleep. If combining with other magnesium forms in a day, space doses by at least 6 hours.

supplements.name: Zinc
supplements.shortDescription: Essential mineral for immunity, testosterone production, and wound healing.

timing.reason: Can cause nausea on an empty stomach. Evening with food avoids competition with copper and other minerals.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Zinc and magnesium together (ZMA stack) support testosterone levels and sleep quality.
synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Zinc supports Vitamin D receptor function and immune signaling.

interactionReasons.iron: Iron and zinc compete for the same intestinal transporter (DMT1) — high-dose competition significantly reduces absorption of both. Take at least 2 hours apart. (Sandström B, J Nutr 1985; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium competes with zinc for absorption via shared ZIP transporters. Space doses by at least 2 hours. (Wood RJ, Am J Clin Nutr 1997; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.folate: Chronic high-dose zinc supplementation (≥40 mg/day) can impair folate absorption and reduce serum folate levels. Monitor folate status if taking both long-term. (Milne DB, Am J Clin Nutr 1984)
interactionReasons.magnesium_citrate: High-dose magnesium (>350 mg elemental) competes with zinc for divalent mineral transporter absorption. At typical supplement doses the effect is minor; space by 1–2 hours if taking therapeutic doses of either. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.copper: High-dose zinc induces metallothionein, which traps copper in the gut and can lead to severe copper deficiency. A 10:1 or 15:1 zinc-to-copper ratio is recommended.

supplements.notes: Do not take with calcium or iron — they compete for absorption. UL is 40 mg/day. Long-term supplementation >40 mg may deplete copper; consider a copper supplement. Picolinate form is well-absorbed.
sources: Prasad AS, J Am Coll Nutr 1996

supplements.name: Iron
supplements.shortDescription: Essential mineral for oxygen transport, energy, and cognitive function.

timing.reason: Best absorbed on an empty stomach. Pair with Vitamin C to enhance absorption. Avoid with calcium, coffee, or tea.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C reduces ferric to ferrous iron, significantly boosting non-heme iron absorption — up to 3-fold.

interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium reduces non-heme iron absorption by up to 60% at doses ≥300 mg. Take iron and calcium at least 2 hours apart. (Hallberg L, Am J Clin Nutr 1991; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.zinc: Iron and zinc compete for the same intestinal transporter (DMT1) — high-dose competition significantly reduces absorption of both. Take at least 2 hours apart. (Sandström B, J Nutr 1985; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium competes with iron for divalent metal intestinal transporters. Space by at least 2 hours for optimal iron absorption. (Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998)
interactionReasons.magnesium_malate: Magnesium competes with iron for divalent metal intestinal transporters. Space by at least 2 hours for optimal iron absorption. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_threonate: Magnesium (all forms) competes with iron at intestinal absorption level. Space by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_citrate: Magnesium competes with iron for divalent metal transporter (DMT1) absorption. Take iron and magnesium at least 2 hours apart, particularly important when treating iron deficiency. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_taurate: Magnesium (all forms) competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters (DMT1). Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS; Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998)
interactionReasons.magnesium_oxide: Magnesium (all forms) competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters. Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.curcumin: Curcumin forms insoluble iron complexes (iron chelation) in the gut, significantly reducing iron absorption. Take at least 3 hours apart. (Tuntipopipat S, J Nutr 2006; Jiao Y, Food Chem 2009)
interactionReasons.resveratrol: Resveratrol chelates iron ions, reducing intestinal iron absorption. Particularly important for those supplementing iron for deficiency. Take separately. (Scheers N, Nutrients 2020)
interactionReasons.nac: N-Acetyl Cysteine can chelate iron via thiol groups, forming iron-NAC complexes that reduce iron bioavailability. Space by at least 2 hours. (Haase H, Metallomics 2012)
interactionReasons.prebiotics: Inulin-type fiberals (FOS, inulin) can bind non-heme iron in the gut, reducing absorption by 10–30%. Take iron separately from prebiotic supplements. (Laparra JM, J Nutr 2012)
interactionReasons.vitamin_e: Vitamin E at doses >400 IU/day can reduce iron absorption by inhibiting ferric-to-ferrous conversion at the intestinal brush border. Space by 2 hours, especially critical during iron deficiency treatment. (Lynch SR, Am J Clin Nutr 1980)
interactionReasons.green_tea_extract: EGCG in green tea forms insoluble complexes with iron, preventing its absorption. Space by at least 2 hours.

supplements.commonDoses: RDA: 8 mg/day (men), 18 mg/day (women 19–50), 27 mg/day (pregnant). Supplement only under medical supervision.
supplements.notes: Only supplement if deficient — excess iron is harmful and can cause organ damage. Ferrous bisglycinate is gentler on the digestive system. Avoid with calcium, antacids, coffee, and tea. Do not co-administer with zinc.
sources: Hallberg L, Am J Clin Nutr 1989

supplements.name: Calcium
supplements.shortDescription: Essential for strong bones, muscle contractions, and nerve signaling.

timing.reason: Calcium carbonate requires stomach acid — take with food. Split doses of ≤500 mg for better absorption.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Vitamin D3 is essential for active calcium absorption in the gut (via calbindin).
synergyReasons.vitamin_k2: K2 ensures calcium is deposited in bones rather than soft tissues and arteries.

interactionReasons.iron: Calcium reduces non-heme iron absorption by up to 60% at doses ≥300 mg. Take calcium and iron at least 2 hours apart. (Hallberg L, Am J Clin Nutr 1991; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.zinc: Calcium competes with zinc for intestinal absorption via shared ZIP transporters. Space doses by at least 2 hours. (Wood RJ, Am J Clin Nutr 1997; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Calcium and magnesium compete for the same intestinal absorption channels at high doses. Separate by 2+ hours for optimal absorption of both. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975)
interactionReasons.magnesium_malate: Calcium and magnesium share intestinal transporters — high-dose co-administration reduces absorption of both. Space by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_threonate: Calcium competes with magnesium for intestinal transport. If taking both, separate by 2 hours to avoid mutual absorption interference. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_citrate: Calcium and magnesium compete for shared intestinal absorption channels. High doses of either reduce the other's bioavailability. Separate by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_taurate: Calcium and magnesium compete for shared intestinal absorption channels. High doses of either reduce the other's bioavailability. Separate by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.magnesium_oxide: Calcium and magnesium share intestinal absorption transporters. Even at low absorption rates, high-dose oxide can interfere. Separate by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)

supplements.commonDoses: 500–1,200 mg daily (total from diet + supplement combined; do not exceed 2,500 mg/day)
supplements.notes: Calcium citrate is better absorbed than carbonate and can be taken without food. Do not take with iron or zinc. Most adults get sufficient calcium from dairy — supplementation is primarily for post-menopausal women.

supplements.name: Iodine
supplements.shortDescription: Critical for thyroid hormone production, metabolism, and brain development.

timing.reason: Best taken in the morning with food to support thyroid function without interfering with sleep.

synergyReasons.selenium: Selenium-dependent enzymes protect the thyroid from oxidative damage caused by H₂O₂ produced during iodine metabolism.

supplements.commonDoses: RDA: 150 mcg/day. UL: 1,100 mcg/day. Supplement only if deficient.
supplements.notes: Most people in developed countries get sufficient iodine from iodized salt and dairy. Excess iodine can paradoxically suppress thyroid function (Wolff-Chaikoff effect). Only supplement under guidance.

supplements.name: Selenium
supplements.shortDescription: Antioxidant mineral supporting thyroid function and immune health.

timing.reason: Taken with food to maximize absorption and reduce stomach upset.

synergyReasons.iodine: Selenium protects the thyroid gland from oxidative stress during iodine metabolism — the two minerals are essential partners for thyroid health.

interactionReasons.vitamin_c: High-dose Vitamin C can reduce bioavailability of inorganic selenium (selenate form) by converting it to poorly absorbed elemental selenium. Prefer selenomethionine form or take separately. (Oster O, J Nutr Biochem 1993)

supplements.commonDoses: 55–200 mcg daily (RDA: 55 mcg; UL: 400 mcg — do not exceed)
supplements.notes: Selenium toxicity (selenosis) can occur above 400 mcg/day — hair loss, nail brittleness, and neurological symptoms. Selenomethionine is the preferred organic form. Brazil nuts are a rich dietary source (70–90 mcg each).

supplements.name: Folate (B9)
supplements.shortDescription: Critical for DNA synthesis, methylation, and neural tube development.

timing.reason: Well-absorbed at any time. Morning is preferred for consistency.

synergyReasons.vitamin_b12: B12 and folate are co-dependent for the methylation cycle, DNA synthesis, and homocysteine regulation — deficiency of either impairs the other.

interactionReasons.zinc: Chronic high-dose zinc (≥40 mg/day) can impair folate absorption and lower serum folate levels by competing for intestinal uptake mechanisms. Monitor folate if supplementing high-dose zinc long-term. (Milne DB, Am J Clin Nutr 1984; NIH ODS)

supplements.commonDoses: 400–800 mcg daily (RDA: 400 mcg; 600 mcg during pregnancy)
supplements.notes: Methylfolate (5-MTHF) is preferred over synthetic folic acid, especially for those with MTHFR C677T or A1298C gene variants. Essential during pregnancy to prevent neural tube defects.

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate
supplements.shortDescription: The most researched supplement for strength, power output, and muscle growth.

timing.reason: Timing matters less than daily consistency. Post-workout with carbohydrates may slightly improve muscle uptake.

synergyReasons.magnesium_malate: Both support ATP production and muscular performance through different mechanisms.
synergyReasons.beta_alanine: Creatine boosts peak power; beta-alanine extends endurance — a well-studied pre-workout combination.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Early research suggested caffeine blunts creatine's ability to enhance phosphocreatine resynthesis during repeated high-intensity bouts. While later studies show mixed results, some individuals may see reduced creatine efficacy when co-ingesting caffeine acutely. If stacking both, consider separating doses. (Vandenberghe K, J Appl Physiol 1996; Hespel P, J Physiol 2002)

supplements.notes: No loading phase is necessary for long-term use. Monohydrate is the gold standard — more expensive forms (HCl, buffered, ethyl ester) offer no proven advantage. Safe for long-term use in healthy individuals.
sources: Rawson ES, J Strength Cond Res 2003

supplements.name: Whey Protein
supplements.shortDescription: Fast-absorbing complete protein ideal for muscle repair and growth after training.

timing.reason: Best consumed within 2 hours post-workout or as a convenient protein supplement between meals.

synergyReasons.creatine: Protein + creatine is the most evidence-backed combination for lean muscle accretion.
synergyReasons.collagen_peptides: Whey provides a full amino acid profile; collagen provides glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline for connective tissue.

supplements.notes: Isolate is preferable if lactose-intolerant. Contains all essential amino acids including leucine — the key trigger for muscle protein synthesis.
sources: Phillips SM, J Am Coll Nutr 2011

supplements.name: Casein Protein
supplements.shortDescription: Slow-digesting protein that provides sustained amino acid release — ideal before sleep.

timing.reason: Slow digestion (~7 hours) makes it ideal before sleep to support overnight muscle protein synthesis.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Both support overnight recovery — casein feeds muscles while magnesium supports sleep quality.

supplements.notes: Micellar casein is preferred over calcium caseinate. Research by Res et al. (2012) demonstrated 22% greater whole-body protein synthesis during overnight sleep.
sources: Res PT, Med Sci Sports Exerc 2012

supplements.name: Beta-Alanine
supplements.shortDescription: Increases muscle carnosine to buffer lactic acid and extend high-intensity performance.

timing.reason: Best taken 30–60 minutes before training. Food reduces the tingling sensation (paresthesia).

synergyReasons.creatine: Creatine boosts peak power output; beta-alanine extends time-to-exhaustion — a well-studied pre-workout combination.
synergyReasons.citrulline_malate: Citrulline targets blood flow and aerobic endurance; beta-alanine buffers acid in anaerobic exercise.

supplements.commonDoses: 3.2–6.4 g daily (split into 800 mg–1.6 g doses to minimize paresthesia)
supplements.notes: Tingling/flushing (paresthesia) is a normal, harmless physiological response. Sustained-release formulations reduce this side effect. Benefits accumulate with 4+ weeks of consistent use.

supplements.name: Citrulline Malate
supplements.shortDescription: Boosts nitric oxide production for better blood flow, pumps, and aerobic endurance.

timing.reason: Take 60 minutes before training for optimal nitric oxide elevation during exercise.

synergyReasons.beta_alanine: Citrulline improves blood flow and aerobic capacity; beta-alanine buffers acid — complementary mechanisms for endurance athletes.
synergyReasons.creatine: Citrulline enhances blood flow to muscles; creatine fuels ATP — a classic pre-workout combination.

supplements.commonDoses: 6–8 g citrulline malate (2:1 ratio) before training
supplements.notes: Citrulline malate (2:1) is more researched than pure L-citrulline for performance. More effective than arginine at raising plasma arginine levels, as it bypasses first-pass hepatic metabolism.
sources: Pérez-Guisado J, J Strength Cond Res 2010
Figueroa A, J Strength Cond Res 2015

supplements.name: Electrolytes
supplements.shortDescription: Replenishes minerals lost in sweat to maintain hydration, performance, and prevent cramps.

timing.reason: Take during or after intense exercise to replenish losses. Particularly important in heat or for endurance sports.

synergyReasons.creatine: Creatine draws water into muscle cells — adequate electrolyte and hydration status supports this effect.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium is an electrolyte itself and supports muscle relaxation and cramp prevention.

interactionReasons.iron: Electrolyte blends containing calcium and magnesium significantly reduce non-heme iron absorption when co-ingested. Take iron supplements at least 2 hours apart from electrolyte drinks or tablets. (Hallberg L, Am J Clin Nutr 1991; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.zinc: Calcium and magnesium present in electrolyte formulas compete with zinc for intestinal absorption via shared ZIP transporters. Take zinc separately from electrolyte supplements. (Wood RJ, Am J Clin Nutr 1997)

supplements.commonDoses: Varies by product; ensure adequate sodium (≥500 mg), potassium, and magnesium per serving
supplements.notes: Low-sugar or sugar-free electrolyte formulas are preferable for most people. Regular table salt with foods covers most needs unless exercising intensely for >60 min.
sources: Sawka MN, Med Sci Sports Exerc 2007

supplements.name: Collagen Peptides
supplements.shortDescription: Supports joint health, skin elasticity, tendon repair, and gut lining integrity.

timing.reason: Take with Vitamin C 30–60 minutes before activity to maximize collagen synthesis in joints and tendons.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C is an essential cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase — the enzyme required for collagen crosslinking. Shaw et al. (2017) showed this combination enhances tendon collagen synthesis.

supplements.notes: Not a complete protein — low in tryptophan. Best used alongside other protein sources. Look for hydrolyzed (peptide) form for better absorption.
sources: Shaw G, Am J Clin Nutr 2017

supplements.name: Omega-3 (Fish Oil)
supplements.shortDescription: Essential fatty acids supporting brain function, inflammation reduction, and heart health.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — bioavailability is up to 60% higher when taken with a fat-containing meal. Also reduces fishy aftertaste.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Both are fat-soluble and share absorption pathways — D3 + Omega-3 is a core foundational stack.
synergyReasons.curcumin: Both reduce inflammation via complementary pathways (EPA/DHA via eicosanoids; curcumin via NF-κB).

interactionReasons.iron: Free iron ions catalyze lipid peroxidation of EPA and DHA, degrading the quality and antioxidant capacity of omega-3 supplements. Take iron and fish oil at different times of day to preserve omega-3 integrity. (Siddiqui RA, Prostaglandins Leukot Essent Fatty Acids 2004)

supplements.notes: Look for at least 500 mg combined EPA+DHA per capsule. Triglyceride form is significantly better absorbed than ethyl ester. Third-party tested for oxidation and heavy metals (IFOS certified).

supplements.name: Lion's Mane Mushroom
supplements.shortDescription: Promotes nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis, supporting memory, focus, and neuroplasticity.

timing.reason: Best taken in the morning to support cognitive performance throughout the day.

synergyReasons.omega3: Both support neuroplasticity and brain health through different mechanisms (NGF vs. DHA membrane support).
synergyReasons.alpha_gpc: Alpha-GPC provides acetylcholine precursor support while Lion's Mane promotes NGF — complementary brain stack.

supplements.commonDoses: 500–3,000 mg daily (fruiting body extract standardized for beta-glucans)
supplements.notes: Use fruiting body extracts, not mycelium-on-grain products — the latter have low active compound content. Effects typically noticed after 4–8 weeks of consistent use.
sources: Lai PL, Int J Med Mushrooms 2013

supplements.name: Alpha-GPC
supplements.shortDescription: Increases acetylcholine levels for enhanced memory, focus, and mind-muscle connection.

timing.reason: Best taken 30–60 minutes before cognitive or physical work for peak acetylcholine availability.

synergyReasons.lions_mane: Lion's Mane promotes NGF while Alpha-GPC boosts acetylcholine — complementary cognitive support.
synergyReasons.l_theanine: L-theanine smooths any cognitive overstimulation and promotes calm, focused attention.

supplements.notes: One of the most bioavailable choline sources. Can cause headaches if total choline intake is already very high. Shown to support growth hormone release in acute studies.
sources: Bellar D, J Int Soc Sports Nutr 2015

supplements.name: L-Theanine
supplements.shortDescription: Promotes calm, focused alertness without drowsiness. Classically paired with caffeine.

timing.reason: Can be taken any time. Pairs perfectly with morning caffeine to smooth jitteriness.

synergyReasons.caffeine: L-theanine attenuates caffeine-induced anxiety and jitteriness while preserving focus-enhancing effects. The 2:1 ratio (200 mg theanine : 100 mg caffeine) is well-studied.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Both promote a calm, relaxed state through different pathways (GABA modulation vs. NMDA receptor activity).

supplements.notes: Very safe with no known serious adverse effects. Naturally found in green and black tea (~20–50 mg per cup). Can be used at higher doses (200–400 mg) for sleep support.
sources: Nobre AC, Asia Pac J Clin Nutr 2008

supplements.name: Caffeine
supplements.shortDescription: The world's most widely consumed stimulant — enhances alertness, focus, and physical performance.

timing.reason: Take in the morning or before exercise. Avoid after 2 PM to prevent sleep disruption (half-life: ~5–6 hours).

synergyReasons.l_theanine: L-theanine significantly reduces caffeine-induced anxiety and jitteriness while preserving its focus and performance benefits.

interactionReasons.melatonin: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors and acts as a stimulant, directly counteracting melatonin's sleep-promoting effect. Avoid caffeine within 6–8 hours of taking melatonin.
interactionReasons.vitamin_b1: Caffeine inhibits thiamine (B1) intestinal transport proteins (ThTr1/ThTr2) and increases urinary thiamine excretion. Regular high caffeine intake can contribute to marginal thiamine depletion over time. Space thiamine supplementation from caffeine by at least 1 hour. (Stein J, Am J Physiol 1987; Said HM, Am J Physiol 1999)

supplements.commonDoses: 100–200 mg per dose; limit to 400 mg/day total
supplements.notes: Tolerance develops rapidly with daily use. Cycling off periodically helps maintain effectiveness. Adenosine receptor antagonist — works by blocking sleep-promoting adenosine. Half-life: ~5–6 hours.

supplements.name: Ashwagandha
supplements.shortDescription: Adaptogen that reduces cortisol, supports stress resilience, testosterone, and sleep.

timing.reason: Evening is preferable as it has calming effects that support sleep. Food improves GI tolerance.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Both reduce HPA axis stress response and support sleep quality via complementary mechanisms.
synergyReasons.l_theanine: Together they form a well-rounded anti-stress, pro-relaxation stack.

interactionReasons.berberine: Both ashwagandha and berberine independently lower blood glucose levels. Their combination can produce an additive hypoglycemic effect that may be excessive in individuals with diabetes or those on blood sugar medications. Monitor glucose levels carefully if combining. (Chandrasekhar K, Indian J Psychol Med 2012; Yin J, Metabolism 2008)
interactionReasons.levothyroxine: Ashwagandha may naturally increase T3 and T4 levels. Combining with thyroid medications like levothyroxine could risk hyperthyroidism-like effects.

supplements.commonDoses: 300–600 mg daily (KSM-66 or Sensoril standardized extract)
supplements.notes: KSM-66 (root extract) and Sensoril (root+leaf) are the most clinically studied forms. Effects build over 4–8 weeks. Avoid during pregnancy. May interact with thyroid medications.
sources: Chandrasekhar K, Indian J Psychol Med 2012

supplements.name: Magnesium L-Threonate
supplements.shortDescription: The only magnesium form shown to meaningfully cross the blood-brain barrier, improving memory and sleep.

timing.reason: Take 1–2 hours before sleep to improve sleep onset and cognitive restoration.

synergyReasons.apigenin: Apigenin + Magnesium L-Threonate is a widely referenced sleep stack (Huberman Lab protocol).
synergyReasons.l_glycine: Both promote deep, restorative sleep through complementary mechanisms (brain Mg levels vs. core body temperature reduction).

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium (all forms) competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters. Space by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS; Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium compete for intestinal transporters. High-dose co-administration reduces the absorption of both. Space by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975)

supplements.notes: More expensive than other magnesium forms. Primarily for cognitive and sleep benefits rather than general magnesium repletion. Animal data is strong; human sleep data emerging. If combining with other magnesium forms for deficiency, space doses by at least 6 hours.

supplements.name: Melatonin
supplements.shortDescription: Regulates the circadian sleep-wake cycle. Most effective for jet lag and shifting sleep timing.

timing.reason: Take 30–60 minutes before your desired sleep time in a dim environment.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium supports the physiological relaxation and GABA signaling needed for melatonin to work effectively.
synergyReasons.l_glycine: L-glycine lowers core body temperature and improves sleep architecture — complementary to melatonin's circadian signal.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors and acts as a stimulant, directly counteracting melatonin's sleep-promoting effect. Avoid caffeine within 6–8 hours of taking melatonin.

supplements.commonDoses: 0.3–1 mg (physiological dose); most OTC products contain 3–10 mg (often unnecessarily high)
supplements.notes: Research consistently shows 0.3–1 mg is equally or more effective than high doses for most people. Use the lowest effective dose to avoid morning grogginess and potential suppression of endogenous production. Not recommended for long-term nightly use without medical guidance.

supplements.name: L-Glycine
supplements.shortDescription: Amino acid that lowers core body temperature to facilitate deep, restful sleep.

timing.reason: Take 30–60 minutes before bed. Dissolves easily in water or warm tea.

synergyReasons.magnesium_threonate: Complementary sleep mechanisms — glycine lowers peripheral body temperature; Mg-threonate works centrally on synaptic magnesium.
synergyReasons.apigenin: Together with melatonin and apigenin, forms the Huberman sleep stack — multiple complementary mechanisms.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Caffeine is a stimulant and adenosine receptor antagonist that directly opposes the relaxation and sleep-promoting effects of L-Glycine. Avoid caffeine within 6 hours of taking L-Glycine for sleep. (Bannai M, Sleep Biol Rhythms 2012)

supplements.notes: Very safe at recommended doses. Evidence shows improved next-day alertness and reduced daytime sleepiness in people with restricted sleep. Acts via NMDA receptor co-agonism and peripheral vasodilation.

supplements.name: GABA
supplements.shortDescription: Inhibitory neurotransmitter precursor that may promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.

timing.reason: Take 30–60 minutes before bed for potential relaxing effects.

synergyReasons.l_theanine: L-theanine modulates GABA signaling — together they may have additive relaxation effects.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Both support GABAergic calming pathways through different mechanisms.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Caffeine acts as a CNS stimulant and adenosine receptor antagonist, directly counteracting the calming and sleep-promoting effects of GABA. Taking both together negates the benefit of GABA supplementation. (Boonstra E, Front Psychol 2015)

supplements.notes: Oral GABA has limited blood-brain barrier penetration — direct CNS effects after supplementation are scientifically debated. Peripheral effects (digestive relaxation, vagal nerve stimulation) may account for reported benefits. PharmaGABA form may have slightly better data.

supplements.name: Apigenin
supplements.shortDescription: Natural flavonoid from chamomile that binds GABA-A receptors to promote relaxation and sleep.

timing.reason: Take 30–60 minutes before sleep. Part of the Huberman sleep protocol.

synergyReasons.magnesium_threonate: Part of the Huberman sleep stack: Mg-threonate + Apigenin + L-glycine target different sleep mechanisms.
synergyReasons.l_glycine: Together lower core body temperature and potentiate GABA-A receptor activity for deeper sleep.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Piperine in black pepper inhibits CYP1A2 — the primary enzyme metabolizing caffeine — and apigenin also inhibits CYP1A2. Combined, they can significantly raise caffeine blood levels, increasing the risk of anxiety, palpitations, or insomnia. Avoid caffeine when taking apigenin. (Bhardwaj RK, J Pharmacol Exp Ther 2002; Wolfman C, Pharmacol Biochem Behav 1994)

supplements.notes: Women who are pregnant or actively trying to conceive should consult a doctor — apigenin has mild anti-estrogenic activity at high doses. Generally considered safe at 50 mg.

supplements.name: Probiotics
supplements.shortDescription: Beneficial bacteria that support gut microbiome diversity, digestion, and immune function.

timing.reason: Best taken 30 minutes before a meal — stomach acid is lower before eating, improving bacterial survival to the gut.

synergyReasons.prebiotics: Prebiotics are the food source for probiotic bacteria — a prebiotic + probiotic (synbiotic) combination significantly improves microbiome colonization.
synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Probiotics can enhance the absorption and metabolic benefits of Vitamin D.

supplements.notes: Multi-strain formulas including Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium species are generally preferred. Refrigerated probiotics may have superior viability. Benefits are strain-specific — not all strains work for all conditions.
sources: Hill C, Nat Rev Gastroenterol Hepatol 2014

supplements.name: Prebiotics
supplements.shortDescription: Non-digestible fibers that selectively feed beneficial gut bacteria and promote microbiome diversity.

timing.reason: Best taken with meals to reduce the risk of bloating and gas during initial adaptation.

synergyReasons.probiotics: Prebiotics feed the probiotic bacteria — a prebiotic + probiotic (synbiotic) combination significantly outperforms either alone.

interactionReasons.iron: Inulin-type prebiotic fibers (FOS, inulin) can bind non-heme iron in the gut via chelation, reducing iron absorption by 10–30%. If supplementing iron, take it at least 2 hours apart from prebiotic supplements. (Laparra JM, J Nutr 2012; Tompkins TA, J Dairy Sci 2001)

supplements.notes: Start with 2–3 g and increase gradually over 2–4 weeks — rapid introduction frequently causes bloating and flatulence. GOS (galactooligosaccharides) tends to be better tolerated than inulin.
sources: Gibson GR, Nat Rev Gastroenterol Hepatol 2017

supplements.name: Berberine
supplements.shortDescription: Plant alkaloid that activates AMPK to improve blood sugar regulation and metabolic health.

timing.reason: Take before or with meals to blunt post-meal blood sugar spikes. Split doses reduce GI side effects.

synergyReasons.omega3: Both support metabolic and cardiovascular health via different mechanisms.
synergyReasons.curcumin: Both have anti-inflammatory and metabolic benefits with complementary pathways (AMPK vs. NF-κB).

interactionReasons.vitamin_b12: Berberine activates AMPK and reduces gastric intrinsic factor secretion — the same mechanism by which metformin impairs vitamin B12 absorption. Long-term berberine use may deplete B12; monitor levels and consider supplementing separately. (Hua W, Eur J Med Chem 2020; de Jager J, BMJ 2010)

supplements.notes: Berberine activates AMPK similarly to Metformin in vitro — however, direct clinical comparisons are limited and it is not FDA-approved as a drug. If you take blood sugar medications or are diabetic, consult a doctor before use as berberine can significantly lower blood glucose. May reduce absorption of certain medications via CYP3A4 inhibition.
sources: Zhang Y, J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2008

supplements.name: CoQ10
supplements.shortDescription: Supports mitochondrial ATP production, antioxidant defense, and cardiovascular health.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — bioavailability is significantly higher when taken with a fat-containing meal.

synergyReasons.omega3: Both support cardiovascular health and mitochondrial membrane integrity.
synergyReasons.resveratrol: Resveratrol activates sirtuins while CoQ10 supports mitochondrial function — complementary longevity pathways.

interactionReasons.iron: Free iron ions can catalyze oxidation of CoQ10 (ubiquinol → ubiquinone), reducing the antioxidant activity of the supplement. Take iron and CoQ10 at least 2 hours apart, ideally at different meals. (Crane FL, Mitochondrion 2007)

supplements.notes: Ubiquinol (reduced form) is better absorbed than ubiquinone, especially in people over 40. Essential consideration for those on statin medications — statins inhibit the same pathway used to synthesize CoQ10.
sources: Mortensen SA, JACC Heart Fail 2014 (Q-SYMBIO trial)

supplements.name: Resveratrol
supplements.shortDescription: Polyphenol that activates sirtuin longevity pathways and provides cardiovascular protection.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — best absorbed with a fat-containing meal. Morning is convenient and consistent.

synergyReasons.coq10: CoQ10 supports mitochondrial function; resveratrol activates sirtuins — complementary longevity mechanisms.
synergyReasons.nmn_nr: NMN/NR boosts NAD+ levels; resveratrol activates the sirtuins that depend on NAD+ — a well-studied longevity combination.

interactionReasons.iron: Resveratrol chelates iron ions, forming stable iron-resveratrol complexes that reduce both iron absorption and resveratrol bioavailability. If supplementing iron for deficiency, take them separately. (Scheers N, Nutrients 2020; Joven J, Crit Rev Food Sci 2014)

supplements.notes: Trans-resveratrol is the bioactive form. Oral bioavailability is low due to rapid metabolism — micronized or liposomal forms improve absorption. Human longevity evidence is still emerging; most robust data is preclinical.

supplements.name: NMN / NR (NAD+ Precursors)
supplements.shortDescription: Precursors to NAD+, supporting cellular energy, DNA repair, and longevity signaling.

timing.reason: Morning is preferred as NAD+ is involved in circadian rhythm regulation and daytime energy metabolism.

synergyReasons.resveratrol: Resveratrol activates sirtuins that consume NAD+ — boosting NAD+ with NMN/NR provides the substrate for this reaction.
synergyReasons.coq10: Both support mitochondrial energy production and reduce age-related mitochondrial dysfunction.

interactionReasons.vitamin_b_complex: NMN and NR are metabolized to nicotinamide (a form of niacin/B3). Combined with a B-complex already containing niacin, total nicotinamide load may exceed the tolerable upper limit (35 mg/day for flushing), potentially causing flushing, liver enzyme elevation, or insulin resistance at very high doses. Monitor total niacin intake. (Trammell SA, Nat Commun 2016; NIH ODS niacin UL)

supplements.commonDoses: 250–500 mg NMN or 300 mg NR daily
supplements.notes: Human research is promising but not yet conclusive for longevity claims — most robust data is in animal models. NMN is more expensive; NR (e.g., Tru Niagen) has more published human trials. Both raise blood NAD+ levels in human studies.

supplements.name: Turmeric / Curcumin
supplements.shortDescription: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound from turmeric root.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — best absorbed with fat-containing meals. Piperine dramatically increases bioavailability.

synergyReasons.black_pepper: Piperine inhibits curcumin glucuronidation, increasing its bioavailability by ~20-fold (2,000%) — always combine these two.
synergyReasons.omega3: Both reduce inflammation via complementary pathways (NF-κB inhibition vs. eicosanoid modulation).
synergyReasons.piperine: Piperine increases the systemic bioavailability of curcumin by up to 2000%.

interactionReasons.iron: Curcumin is a potent iron chelator — it forms insoluble complexes with iron in the gut, significantly reducing iron absorption. Particularly relevant for people with iron deficiency or anemia. Take curcumin and iron at least 3 hours apart. (Tuntipopipat S, J Nutr 2006; Jiao Y, Food Chem 2009)

supplements.notes: Plain turmeric powder has very poor bioavailability. Use formulations with piperine (BioPerine), phospholipid complex (Meriva, BCM-95), or nanoparticle delivery systems.

supplements.name: Black Pepper Extract (Piperine)
supplements.shortDescription: Dramatically enhances bioavailability of curcumin and certain other nutrients.

timing.reason: Take alongside the supplements you want to enhance — particularly curcumin.

synergyReasons.curcumin: Piperine inhibits intestinal and hepatic metabolism of curcumin, increasing bioavailability by ~20-fold — always combine these two.
synergyReasons.resveratrol: Piperine can also reduce the first-pass metabolism of resveratrol, potentially improving its absorption.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Piperine potently inhibits CYP1A2 — the primary hepatic enzyme responsible for caffeine metabolism. Combined use can increase caffeine plasma concentrations by 50–100%, significantly raising the risk of caffeine-related side effects (anxiety, tachycardia, insomnia). Use caution when combining. (Bhardwaj RK, J Pharmacol Exp Ther 2002)

supplements.notes: Usually included at 5 mg in curcumin formulations. Stand-alone dosing is rarely needed. High doses (>20 mg) may affect absorption of certain medications — consult a doctor if taking prescription drugs.

supplements.name: Vitamin B Complex
supplements.shortDescription: All eight essential B vitamins supporting energy metabolism, brain function, and stress response.

timing.reason: Take with breakfast — B vitamins are involved in energy metabolism and food reduces the risk of nausea.

synergyReasons.vitamin_b12: B complex provides a full range of B vitamins; standalone B12 is useful for vegans or those with absorption deficits.
synergyReasons.folate: All B vitamins support the methylation cycle — folate and B12 are the most critical co-factors.

interactionReasons.nmn_nr: NMN and NR are metabolized into nicotinamide (niacin/B3). Combined with a B-complex already containing niacin (B3), total nicotinamide intake may exceed safe levels, risking niacin flush, elevated liver enzymes, or impaired glucose control at high doses. Monitor total niacin intake. (Trammell SA, Nat Commun 2016; NIH ODS niacin UL 35 mg/day)

supplements.commonDoses: 1 serving daily (varies by product; follow label)
supplements.notes: Methylated forms (methylfolate, methylcobalamin) are preferred for better bioavailability. Urine may turn bright yellow from riboflavin (B2) — this is normal and harmless.

supplements.name: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine)
supplements.shortDescription: Precursor to glutathione — the body's master antioxidant. Supports liver, lung, and immune health.

timing.reason: Take with a meal to reduce risk of nausea, which can occur on an empty stomach.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Both support glutathione synthesis and act as complementary antioxidants — Vitamin C can regenerate oxidized glutathione.
synergyReasons.coq10: Together they provide comprehensive mitochondrial protection and antioxidant support.

interactionReasons.iron: NAC contains free thiol (sulfhydryl) groups that chelate iron, forming iron-NAC complexes in the gut. This reduces iron bioavailability — particularly important for people supplementing iron for deficiency. Space by at least 2 hours. (Haase H, Metallomics 2012; Aldini G, Free Radic Res 2018)

supplements.notes: One of the most effective glutathione precursors. Used medically for acetaminophen overdose and respiratory conditions. Note: the FDA attempted to restrict NAC as a supplement in 2020 — regulatory status may vary by region. Consult current local regulations.

supplements.name: Vitamin E
supplements.shortDescription: Fat-soluble antioxidant that protects cells from oxidative damage and supports immune and skin health.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble — requires dietary fat for proper absorption. Take with the largest meal of the day.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C regenerates oxidized Vitamin E, extending its antioxidant activity in cell membranes.
synergyReasons.selenium: Selenium (as glutathione peroxidase) and Vitamin E work synergistically to neutralise lipid peroxides.
synergyReasons.omega3: Vitamin E protects omega-3 fatty acids from oxidation both in the supplement and in cell membranes.

interactionReasons.iron: Vitamin E can inhibit iron absorption when taken simultaneously. Space by at least 2 hours. (Lynch SR, Am J Clin Nutr 1980)
interactionReasons.vitamin_k2: High-dose Vitamin E (>300 IU/day) competitively inhibits vitamin K-dependent γ-carboxylase enzymes, reducing K2's ability to activate osteocalcin and matrix Gla protein. Keep Vitamin E doses moderate (<200 IU) if taking K2 for bone or cardiovascular benefits. (Booth SL, Am J Clin Nutr 2004)

supplements.notes: Choose 'mixed tocopherols' (alpha, beta, gamma, delta) over synthetic dl-alpha-tocopherol for better biological activity. High doses (>400 IU/day) may increase bleeding risk in those on anticoagulants.

supplements.name: Vitamin A
supplements.shortDescription: Essential for vision, immune defence, skin cell turnover, and reproductive health.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble vitamin — absorption is significantly improved when taken with a fat-containing meal.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Vitamins A and D share nuclear receptor pathways and work together to regulate immune function and gene expression.
synergyReasons.zinc: Zinc is required for the synthesis of retinol-binding protein, which transports Vitamin A in the blood.
synergyReasons.vitamin_e: Vitamin E protects Vitamin A from oxidation in the gut and tissues.

interactionReasons.vitamin_d3: High-dose preformed Vitamin A (retinol, >5,000 IU/day) antagonises Vitamin D receptor signalling and has been associated with reduced bone mineral density despite adequate D3 supplementation. Keep the A:D ratio balanced — estimated optimal ratio is roughly 5:1 (IU). Use beta-carotene form to reduce this risk. (Johansson S, J Bone Miner Res 2004; Cannell JJ, Ann Otol Rhinol Laryngol 2008)

supplements.commonDoses: 2,500–5,000 IU daily (retinol); up to 25,000 IU as beta-carotene
supplements.notes: Preformed Vitamin A (retinol) can accumulate and become toxic at high doses — do not exceed 10,000 IU/day long-term. Beta-carotene is converted to Vitamin A as needed and is safer. Pregnant women should avoid high-dose retinol supplementation.

supplements.name: Vitamin B6
supplements.shortDescription: Cofactor in over 100 enzyme reactions including neurotransmitter synthesis, hormone regulation, and amino acid metabolism.

timing.reason: Water-soluble — take with breakfast to reduce risk of nausea and support daytime neurotransmitter activity.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: B6 is required to transport magnesium into cells; the two work together to support sleep, mood, and the nervous system.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b12: B6, B12, and folate form the core homocysteine-lowering trio — all three are needed to convert homocysteine to safe metabolites.
synergyReasons.folate: B6 and folate act together in one-carbon metabolism, supporting DNA synthesis and methylation.

supplements.commonDoses: 10–50 mg daily (P5P form preferred for direct activity)
supplements.notes: P5P (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) is the active coenzyme form and does not require liver conversion. Doses above 100 mg/day long-term have been associated with peripheral neuropathy. Most people get adequate B6 from diet; supplementation is most beneficial for those with high protein intake or stress.

supplements.name: Biotin
supplements.shortDescription: B-vitamin essential for hair, nail, and skin integrity, and for metabolising carbohydrates, fats, and proteins.

timing.reason: Water-soluble — can be taken with or without food. Morning dosing supports daytime metabolic activity.

synergyReasons.zinc: Both support keratin structure; deficiency of either leads to brittle hair and nails.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b_complex: Biotin works synergistically with other B vitamins in energy and macronutrient metabolism.

supplements.notes: High-dose biotin (>5 mg/day) can interfere with thyroid and troponin lab tests, causing false results. Inform your doctor if supplementing biotin before blood tests. True biotin deficiency is rare but more common in those consuming raw egg whites regularly (avidin blocks biotin absorption).

supplements.name: Magnesium Citrate
supplements.shortDescription: Highly bioavailable magnesium form that also supports bowel regularity and digestive comfort.

timing.reason: Evening dosing supports relaxation and sleep. Can be taken without food; may be preferred at bedtime for its mild laxative effect.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Magnesium is required to activate Vitamin D — without sufficient magnesium, D3 supplementation is less effective.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b6: B6 facilitates magnesium transport into cells, enhancing its intracellular activity.

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium competes with iron for divalent metal transporter (DMT1) absorption in the gut. Take iron and magnesium at least 2 hours apart, particularly when treating iron deficiency. (NIH ODS; Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium compete for shared intestinal absorption channels. High doses of either reduce the other's bioavailability. Separate by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975; NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.zinc: High-dose magnesium (>350 mg elemental) competes with zinc for divalent mineral transporter absorption. At typical supplement doses the effect is minor; space by 1–2 hours if taking therapeutic doses of either. (NIH ODS)

supplements.notes: Citrate form has good bioavailability and a mild osmotic laxative effect, making it useful for those with constipation. For those prone to loose stools, magnesium glycinate or malate may be better tolerated. Count elemental magnesium (not total salt weight) when dosing. If taking multiple magnesium forms in a day, space doses by at least 6 hours.
sources: Schuchardt JP, Curr Nutr Food Sci 2017

supplements.name: Magnesium Taurate
supplements.shortDescription: Combines magnesium with taurine for cardiovascular health, blood pressure regulation, and blood sugar support.

timing.reason: Take in the evening with food — food increases magnesium absorption by up to 40% and reduces stomach discomfort.

synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Magnesium is required to activate Vitamin D — without sufficient magnesium, D3 supplementation is less effective.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b6: Vitamin B6 enhances magnesium transport into cells, increasing intracellular magnesium levels.
synergyReasons.l_theanine: Both taurine (in magnesium taurate) and L-theanine support calm and relaxation through complementary GABAergic mechanisms.

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters (DMT1). Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS; Minihane AM, Br J Nutr 1998)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium compete for shared intestinal absorption channels. High doses of either reduce the other's bioavailability. Separate by at least 2 hours. (Seelig MS, JAMA 1975; NIH ODS)

supplements.notes: The taurine component provides additional cardiovascular and neurological support. Caution: may potentiate the effects of digoxin (risk of toxicity — consult your cardiologist). Macrolide antibiotics (e.g. azithromycin, clarithromycin) should be taken at least 2 hours apart from magnesium. If taking multiple magnesium forms in a day, space doses by at least 6 hours.

supplements.name: Magnesium Oxide
supplements.shortDescription: Very low bioavailability magnesium form used primarily as a laxative, not for deficiency correction.

timing.reason: Take in the evening — primarily used for its osmotic laxative effect. Food co-administration is optional since absorption is minimal regardless.

interactionReasons.iron: Magnesium competes with iron at intestinal divalent metal transporters. Space iron supplementation by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)
interactionReasons.calcium: Calcium and magnesium compete for shared intestinal absorption channels. Separate by at least 2 hours. (NIH ODS)

supplements.notes: Only ~4% of elemental magnesium in oxide form is absorbed — the lowest bioavailability of all magnesium salts. Not recommended for correcting magnesium deficiency. Choose glycinate, malate, or citrate instead for repletion. Oxide is only suitable as a cost-effective osmotic laxative for occasional constipation relief.
sources: Lindberg JS, J Am Coll Nutr 1990

supplements.name: Vitamin B1 (Thiamine)
supplements.shortDescription: Essential coenzyme for converting carbohydrates into energy. Critical for nerve and heart function.

timing.reason: Water-soluble — take with breakfast to support energy metabolism throughout the day.

synergyReasons.vitamin_b_complex: All B vitamins work together in energy metabolism; B1 is most effective as part of a complete B-complex.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium is a cofactor for thiamine-dependent enzymes — the two work together in ATP production.

interactionReasons.caffeine: Caffeine inhibits thiamine (B1) intestinal transport proteins (ThTr1/ThTr2) and increases urinary thiamine excretion. Regular high caffeine intake can contribute to marginal thiamine depletion over time. Space thiamine supplementation from caffeine by at least 1 hour. (Said HM, Am J Physiol 1999; Stein J, Am J Physiol 1987)

supplements.commonDoses: 50–100 mg daily (standard); 150–600 mg for benfotiamine (fat-soluble form)
supplements.notes: Benfotiamine is a fat-soluble synthetic derivative of B1 with superior bioavailability and is particularly studied for diabetic neuropathy and metabolic support. Thiamine deficiency is associated with alcohol overuse, restrictive diets, and bariatric surgery.
sources: Thornalley PJ, Ann N Y Acad Sci 2005

supplements.name: St. John's Wort
supplements.shortDescription: Herbal supplement often used for mood and depression.
supplements.notes: Strong inducer of CYP3A4, interacting with many medications and supplements.

interactionReasons.vitamin_d3: St. John's Wort strongly induces CYP3A4, which can accelerate the metabolism of Vitamin D and reduce its serum levels.

supplements.name: Copper
supplements.shortDescription: Essential trace mineral for energy and iron metabolism.
supplements.notes: Avoid taking at the same time as zinc.

interactionReasons.zinc: High-dose zinc can trap copper in the gut, leading to deficiency. A balanced ratio is necessary.

supplements.name: Green Tea Extract (EGCG)
supplements.shortDescription: Powerful antioxidant known for metabolic support.
supplements.notes: Do not take closely with iron supplements.

interactionReasons.iron: EGCG in green tea binds to iron, reducing the absorption of both. Take at least 2 hours apart.

supplements.name: Levothyroxine (Thyroid Med)
supplements.shortDescription: Medication for treating hypothyroidism.
supplements.notes: Take strictly on an empty stomach away from minerals.

interactionReasons.ashwagandha: Ashwagandha may naturally increase T3 and T4 levels. Combining with thyroid medications could risk hyperthyroidism-like effects.
interactionReasons.calcium: Minerals block levothyroxine absorption. Space 4 hours.
interactionReasons.iron: Minerals block levothyroxine absorption. Space 4 hours.
interactionReasons.zinc: Minerals block levothyroxine absorption. Space 4 hours.
interactionReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Minerals block levothyroxine absorption. Space 4 hours.

supplements.name: L-Carnitine
supplements.shortDescription: Transports fatty acids into mitochondria for energy production; supports cardiovascular health and exercise recovery.

timing.reason: Best taken 30–60 minutes before exercise or in the morning fasted; food reduces peak plasma levels.

synergyReasons.coq10: Both support mitochondrial energy production via complementary mechanisms — carnitine shuttles fatty acids while CoQ10 powers the electron transport chain.
synergyReasons.omega3: Omega-3 fatty acids enhance carnitine's fat-oxidation effects and reduce exercise-induced inflammation.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b5: Pantothenic acid (B5) is needed for CoA synthesis, which carnitine depends on for its transport function.

interactionReasons.iron: L-Carnitine biosynthesis requires iron as a cofactor; iron deficiency reduces endogenous carnitine. Not a dangerous interaction — ensure adequate iron intake.

supplements.notes: L-Carnitine Tartrate is best for exercise performance; Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR) is preferred for cognitive benefits. Fish-heavy diets provide some dietary carnitine.

supplements.name: Rhodiola Rosea
supplements.shortDescription: Adaptogenic herb that reduces cortisol, combats fatigue, and enhances mental and physical performance under stress.

timing.reason: Stimulating — best taken in the morning or early afternoon on an empty stomach to avoid sleep disruption.

synergyReasons.ashwagandha: Complementary adaptogens: Rhodiola is energizing and acute-acting; Ashwagandha is calming and long-term. Together they cover the full stress-resilience spectrum.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b_complex: B vitamins support the adrenal glands and nervous system, amplifying Rhodiola's anti-fatigue effects.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium calms the nervous system while Rhodiola boosts resilience — a balanced stress-management stack.

supplements.notes: Cycle 5 days on, 2 days off for best results. Start low (200 mg) and titrate up. May cause restlessness if taken too late in the day.

supplements.name: Quercetin
supplements.shortDescription: Potent plant flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and senolytic properties — clears damaged cells to support healthy aging.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble flavonoid — food (especially with some fat) significantly increases bioavailability.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C regenerates oxidized quercetin back to its active form, extending its antioxidant activity — a powerful synergistic pair.
synergyReasons.resveratrol: Both are polyphenols with complementary longevity mechanisms: quercetin is senolytic, resveratrol activates sirtuins.
synergyReasons.zinc: Quercetin acts as a zinc ionophore, helping zinc enter cells more efficiently — critical for immune defense.
synergyReasons.fisetin: Both are senolytics that clear senescent cells — combining them at lower doses may reduce side effects while maintaining efficacy.

supplements.notes: Quercetin Phytosome (with sunflower phospholipids) has 20x better bioavailability than standard quercetin. Used in senolytic protocols at 1,000 mg pulsed 2x/week.

supplements.name: 5-HTP (Griffonia)
supplements.shortDescription: Direct serotonin precursor that improves mood, reduces anxiety, and promotes deeper sleep without dependence.

timing.reason: Evening on an empty stomach for sleep benefits — serotonin converts to melatonin overnight.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium is a cofactor in the enzymatic conversion of 5-HTP to serotonin, amplifying its effect.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b6: B6 (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) is essential for aromatic amino acid decarboxylase — the enzyme that converts 5-HTP to serotonin.
synergyReasons.l_theanine: L-Theanine promotes GABA-mediated calm while 5-HTP elevates serotonin — together they address both anxious and depressive components of sleep disruption.

interactionReasons.st_johns_wort: Both increase serotonin availability — combining them risks serotonin syndrome (agitation, confusion, rapid heart rate). Do not combine without medical supervision.
interactionReasons.nac: NAC increases glutathione, which can modulate serotonin metabolism. The interaction is mild but monitor for mood changes.

supplements.notes: Start with 50–100 mg to assess tolerance. Do not combine with SSRIs, SNRIs, or MAOIs without physician guidance. Short-term (8–12 week) cycles are safer than continuous use.

supplements.name: Glucosamine Sulfate
supplements.shortDescription: Naturally occurring compound that rebuilds cartilage, reduces joint pain, and may slow osteoarthritis progression.

timing.reason: Take with a meal to reduce gastrointestinal upset and improve tolerability.

synergyReasons.msm: MSM provides sulfur needed for cartilage formation while glucosamine provides the structural building blocks — the classic joint supplement combination.
synergyReasons.omega3: Omega-3 reduces joint inflammation while glucosamine rebuilds cartilage — addressing both cause and structure.
synergyReasons.collagen_peptides: Collagen provides the matrix framework for cartilage while glucosamine supplies the proteoglycan building blocks.
synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis in cartilage and enhances glucosamine's structural repair effects.

supplements.notes: Glucosamine sulfate (not HCl) is the clinically validated form. Allow 6–8 weeks to notice joint benefits. Derived from shellfish — choose plant-based (fermented corn) if allergic.

supplements.name: Myo-Inositol
supplements.shortDescription: Cellular messenger that improves insulin sensitivity, supports mood (PCOS, OCD, anxiety), and regulates reproductive hormones.

timing.reason: Morning on an empty stomach for consistent absorption — split dose (morning and evening) is common in PCOS protocols.

synergyReasons.vitamin_b_complex: B vitamins support inositol's role in cellular signaling and nerve function.
synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Magnesium and inositol both influence GABA and serotonin pathways — complementary mood and anxiety support.
synergyReasons.folate: Folate and inositol are both critical for methylation and cell growth — important in PCOS and fertility contexts.

supplements.commonDoses: 2,000–4,000 mg daily (often in 4:1 Myo:D-Chiro ratio for PCOS)
supplements.notes: Myo-Inositol + D-Chiro-Inositol at 40:1 ratio is the evidence-based combination for PCOS and insulin resistance. High doses (12g+) may cause mild GI upset.
sources: Costantino D, Eur Rev Med Pharmacol 2009

supplements.name: Ginkgo Biloba
supplements.shortDescription: Ancient tree extract that improves circulation to the brain, enhances memory and processing speed, and reduces age-related cognitive decline.

timing.reason: Take with food to improve tolerability; split into 2 doses (morning/noon) for stable blood levels.

synergyReasons.alpha_gpc: Alpha-GPC increases acetylcholine synthesis while Ginkgo improves cerebral blood flow — addressing both the neurotransmitter and delivery aspects of cognition.
synergyReasons.bacopa_monnieri: Bacopa enhances memory consolidation while Ginkgo improves processing speed and circulation — complementary cognitive enhancers.
synergyReasons.omega3: Omega-3 supports neuronal membrane integrity while Ginkgo improves cerebral blood flow — synergistic for long-term brain health.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b12: B12 deficiency impairs nerve conduction; combining with Ginkgo's circulation benefits amplifies cognitive protection.

interactionReasons.vitamin_e: Both Ginkgo and high-dose Vitamin E have mild blood-thinning effects — combining may increase bleeding risk. Space them and keep Vitamin E under 200 IU.
interactionReasons.omega3: High-dose omega-3 (>3g EPA+DHA) combined with Ginkgo may have additive antiplatelet effects. Moderate doses are generally safe; caution before surgery.

supplements.commonDoses: 120–240 mg daily (standardized to 24% ginkgo flavonoids, 6% terpene lactones)
supplements.notes: Allow 4–6 weeks for cognitive effects. Avoid before surgery (antiplatelet effect). Use standardized extract (EGb 761 or LI 1370) — raw leaf teas have inconsistent dosing.

supplements.name: Elderberry (Sambucus)
supplements.shortDescription: Anthocyanin-rich berry that reduces cold and flu severity and duration by inhibiting viral replication and boosting immune response.

timing.reason: Take with food; daily maintenance dose in the morning, higher doses at first sign of illness.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C and elderberry anthocyanins provide complementary antioxidant and antiviral immune support.
synergyReasons.zinc: Zinc inhibits viral replication while elderberry prevents viral attachment to cells — dual-mechanism immune defense.
synergyReasons.vitamin_d3: Vitamin D3 modulates innate immunity while elderberry provides acute antiviral activity — together they cover both long-term and immediate immune protection.
synergyReasons.probiotics: Gut microbiome health is central to immune function; probiotics enhance elderberry's systemic immune benefits.

supplements.commonDoses: 150–300 mg extract (standardized to 25% anthocyanins) or 10–15 ml syrup daily
supplements.notes: Clinical evidence strongest for reducing flu severity and duration by 2–4 days. Raw, unripe elderberries contain cyanogenic glycosides — only use properly processed commercial preparations.
sources: Zakay-Rones Z, J Int Med Res 2004

supplements.name: Milk Thistle (Silymarin)
supplements.shortDescription: Liver-protective herb that promotes liver cell regeneration, reduces inflammation, and supports detoxification pathways.

timing.reason: Take with food to improve silymarin absorption (fat-soluble) and minimize gastrointestinal irritation.

synergyReasons.nac: NAC raises intracellular glutathione while silymarin protects liver cells from oxidative damage — powerful liver detox stack.
synergyReasons.vitamin_e: Both are antioxidants that protect liver cell membranes from lipid peroxidation — complementary hepatoprotective effects.
synergyReasons.curcumin: Curcumin reduces liver inflammation while milk thistle promotes hepatocyte regeneration — addressing different aspects of liver protection.
synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C regenerates antioxidants used by silymarin, sustaining liver protection.

supplements.notes: Most beneficial with regular alcohol use, medication-induced liver stress, or NAFLD. Phytosome form (silymarin-phosphatidylcholine complex) has 4–10x better bioavailability.

supplements.name: Taurine
supplements.shortDescription: Conditionally essential amino acid supporting heart function, neurological health, exercise performance, and mitochondrial efficiency.

timing.reason: Morning or 1 hour before exercise fasted — taken with food slows absorption but improves GI tolerance.

synergyReasons.magnesium_glycinate: Taurine and magnesium have synergistic cardiovascular and neurological effects — this is why magnesium taurate exists as a combined form.
synergyReasons.coq10: Both support mitochondrial efficiency and heart function via complementary mechanisms.
synergyReasons.l_carnitine: Carnitine handles fatty acid transport; taurine supports mitochondrial membrane potential — together they maximize cellular energy production.
synergyReasons.electrolytes: Taurine is a key regulator of cellular electrolyte balance (sodium, potassium, calcium) — supports and amplifies electrolyte supplementation.

supplements.notes: One of the few supplements shown to improve longevity in animal models. Very safe at doses up to 3g/day — doses in energy drinks (80–1000 mg) are pharmacologically active. Vegans have lower taurine status.

supplements.name: Bacopa Monnieri
supplements.shortDescription: Ayurvedic herb that enhances memory formation, speeds information processing, and reduces anxiety through neurotrophic and antioxidant mechanisms.

timing.reason: Always take with fat-containing food — bacosides are fat-soluble and bioavailability drops sharply without dietary fat.

synergyReasons.lions_mane: Lion's Mane promotes NGF (nerve growth) while Bacopa enhances synaptic communication and memory encoding — complementary neuroplasticity stack.
synergyReasons.alpha_gpc: Alpha-GPC boosts acetylcholine synthesis while Bacopa inhibits acetylcholinesterase (slows breakdown) — synergistic cholinergic enhancement.
synergyReasons.ginkgo_biloba: Ginkgo improves cerebral blood flow while Bacopa enhances memory consolidation — addressing delivery and processing together.
synergyReasons.omega3: DHA in omega-3 supports neuronal membrane fluidity while Bacopa's bacosides enhance synaptic plasticity.

supplements.notes: Requires 8–12 weeks of consistent use for full memory benefits — unlike stimulants, effects build gradually. May cause GI upset if taken without food. Most studied at 300 mg of the KM Syriva KeenMind/Naturex extract.
sources: Calabrese C, J Altern Complement Med 2008

supplements.name: Phosphatidylserine (PS)
supplements.shortDescription: Key phospholipid in neuronal membranes that improves memory, reduces cortisol, and supports ADHD and age-related cognitive decline.

timing.reason: Best absorbed with fat-containing food; split into 2–3 doses with meals for stable blood levels.

synergyReasons.omega3: DHA and EPA from omega-3 are incorporated alongside phosphatidylserine in neuronal membranes — they are structurally interdependent.
synergyReasons.alpha_gpc: Alpha-GPC supplies the choline backbone for membrane phospholipids while PS provides the serine head — both are essential structural components for optimal neuronal function.
synergyReasons.bacopa_monnieri: Bacopa's bacosides enhance membrane fluidity and synaptic efficiency while PS ensures optimal membrane structure.
synergyReasons.ashwagandha: Both reduce cortisol — ashwagandha via HPA axis modulation, PS via direct blunting of cortisol response to exercise and cognitive stress.

supplements.notes: Sharp-PS (sunflower lecithin-derived) is preferred over soy-derived PS. FDA-qualified health claim for reducing dementia risk at ≥100 mg/day. Well-studied in children with ADHD.

supplements.name: Fisetin
supplements.shortDescription: Potent senolytic flavonoid found in strawberries that clears senescent cells, crosses the blood-brain barrier, and activates longevity pathways.

timing.reason: Fat-containing food dramatically improves bioavailability of this fat-soluble flavonoid.

synergyReasons.quercetin: Both are senolytics — combining them covers different cell populations and may achieve clearance at lower individual doses, reducing potential side effects.
synergyReasons.resveratrol: Resveratrol activates SIRT1/AMPK longevity pathways while fisetin clears accumulated senescent cells — structural and functional longevity synergy.
synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C regenerates oxidized fisetin and enhances its bioavailability and duration of action.
synergyReasons.black_pepper: Piperine in black pepper inhibits fisetin's first-pass metabolism, increasing blood levels by 20–30%.

supplements.commonDoses: 100–200 mg daily continuous, or 500–1,500 mg in 2-day senolytic pulses
supplements.notes: Senolytic protocol (Kirkland et al.): 1,000–1,500 mg for 2 consecutive days/month. Most concentrated food source: strawberries (160 mcg/g). Longevity Bioscience Fisetin uses a phospholipid complex for better absorption.

supplements.name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid (ALA)
supplements.shortDescription: Universal antioxidant that regenerates vitamins C and E, improves insulin sensitivity, reduces nerve damage, and chelates heavy metals.

timing.reason: Fasted absorption is significantly higher — take 30 minutes before meals for maximum bioavailability.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: ALA directly regenerates oxidized vitamin C back to its active ascorbate form — among the most potent antioxidant recycling synergies.
synergyReasons.vitamin_e: ALA regenerates vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol) from its oxidized form, extending its protective duration.
synergyReasons.coq10: Both support mitochondrial energy production and membrane protection — synergistic against mitochondrial dysfunction.
synergyReasons.nac: NAC raises glutathione while ALA both recycles glutathione and independently scavenges free radicals — comprehensive antioxidant defense.
synergyReasons.vitamin_b1: ALA functions as a cofactor with thiamine (B1) in the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex, making them metabolically interdependent.

supplements.commonDoses: 200–600 mg daily (R-ALA preferred over racemic ALA)
supplements.notes: R-ALA is the biologically active form with 3–5x better potency. Na-R-ALA (sodium salt) is stabilized and more bioavailable. ALA mildly lowers blood sugar — diabetics should monitor glucose closely.

supplements.name: Astaxanthin
supplements.shortDescription: Marine carotenoid 6,000x more potent than vitamin C as an antioxidant — protects skin, eyes, muscles, and mitochondria from oxidative damage.

timing.reason: Fat-soluble carotenoid — bioavailability increases 3-7x when taken with fat-containing food.

synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C works in aqueous environments while astaxanthin protects lipid membranes — together they provide complete cellular antioxidant coverage.
synergyReasons.vitamin_e: Both are lipid-soluble antioxidants that protect cell membranes — astaxanthin spans the entire membrane bilayer while vitamin E protects only the outer layer.
synergyReasons.omega3: Omega-3 fatty acids increase the risk of lipid peroxidation; astaxanthin specifically protects polyunsaturated fatty acids from oxidation — a natural pairing.
synergyReasons.coq10: Both protect mitochondrial membranes and support energy production — synergistic mitochondrial antioxidant coverage.

supplements.notes: Most concentrated natural source is Haematococcus pluvialis microalgae. Skin benefits (UV protection, elasticity) appear at 4 mg/day after 4–8 weeks. Unlike beta-carotene, no toxicity concern at standard doses.

supplements.name: Spermidine
supplements.shortDescription: Natural polyamine that induces autophagy (cellular self-cleaning), linked to reduced cardiovascular mortality and cognitive preservation with aging.

timing.reason: Morning on an empty stomach to maximize autophagy induction — food (especially protein) blunts the fasting-mimetic effect.

synergyReasons.resveratrol: Resveratrol activates SIRT1 while spermidine activates autophagy via TFEB — complementary cellular rejuvenation mechanisms.
synergyReasons.quercetin: Quercetin is senolytic (clears damaged cells) while spermidine induces autophagy (cleans within cells) — together they address cellular aging comprehensively.
synergyReasons.nmn_nr: NMN/NR restores NAD+ for energy metabolism while spermidine stimulates autophagy — both are core interventions in longevity research.
synergyReasons.fisetin: Fisetin clears senescent cells while spermidine activates the cell's internal cleaning system — synergistic anti-aging from different angles.

supplements.commonDoses: 1–5 mg daily (often as wheat germ extract)
supplements.notes: Food sources: wheat germ (highest), aged cheese, soybeans, mushrooms. Longevity research (Madeo et al.) suggests dietary spermidine intake correlates inversely with cardiovascular mortality. Most commercial supplements use wheat germ extract.

supplements.name: MSM (Methylsulfonylmethane)
supplements.shortDescription: Organic sulfur compound that reduces joint inflammation, muscle soreness, supports collagen synthesis, and enhances antioxidant capacity.

timing.reason: Take with food to reduce GI discomfort; splitting into morning and evening doses improves tolerability.

synergyReasons.glucosamine: MSM provides sulfur for proteoglycan synthesis while glucosamine provides the backbone — the evidence-based joint supplement duo.
synergyReasons.collagen_peptides: Both contribute sulfur-containing amino acids (cysteine in collagen; MSM as sulfur source) needed for collagen cross-linking and cartilage integrity.
synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C is essential for collagen hydroxylation while MSM provides the sulfur — together they support complete connective tissue repair.
synergyReasons.omega3: Omega-3 reduces joint inflammation while MSM inhibits pro-inflammatory signaling — addressing joint pain from both inflammatory and structural angles.

supplements.notes: Allow 4–6 weeks before assessing joint benefits. OptiMSM is the most researched and purified commercial form. Also used for skin and hair benefits via sulfur's role in keratin and collagen.

supplements.name: Hyaluronic Acid
supplements.shortDescription: Natural glycosaminoglycan that lubricates joints, hydrates skin from within, and reduces osteoarthritis pain by restoring synovial fluid.

timing.reason: Take with food for best absorption; oral HA is absorbed via intestinal HA-receptors that are more active in fed state.

synergyReasons.collagen_peptides: Collagen provides structural protein while hyaluronic acid provides hydration and lubrication — comprehensive skin and joint matrix support.
synergyReasons.vitamin_c: Vitamin C is essential for both collagen and hyaluronic acid synthesis in connective tissue.
synergyReasons.glucosamine: Glucosamine stimulates HA production in synovial cells while supplemental HA provides immediate lubrication.
synergyReasons.msm: MSM reduces inflammation while HA restores joint lubrication — addressing both symptoms and structure of joint degradation.

supplements.notes: Low molecular weight HA (< 50 kDa) has better oral bioavailability. High molecular weight HA is preferred for injectable treatments. Skin hydration effects visible in 4–8 weeks. Also improves gum health.

supplements.name: Reishi Mushroom (Ganoderma)
